item.content:encoded:
Woo! This is the first article I've worked on to crack a hundred upvotes!

Thanks again to Zyn, my fantastic co-author, and everyone that has read this thing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A little late, but the scraping in my mind is just to make things more unsettling. While bones don't make squicky scraping sounds in most cases, they can as a result of some unpleasant conditions and injuries. I thought it would be weird if there was an object that made you hear bones make awful scraping sounds, and (with Zyn's help) the idea snowballed from there.

The original title for this SCP was "Creepitation" which is a play on crepitation, which Merriam-Webster defines as

a grating or crackling sound or sensation (as that produced by the fractured ends of a bone moving against each other or as that in tissues affected with gas gangrene)

Nasty, eh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the upvote!

I'm admittedly not certain about the scraping (Lesh can probably explain it, since that was a portion written by them), but I headcanon it as the sound of bones whispering as they get in the mood. Or something to set the mood for the unsettling bit. But that's just me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In retrospect, maybe. Dang. That's a good point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, a really good, weird, and in general entertaining read.

My only nitpick is the "loud scraping" part. Your bone joints have connective tissue padding that specifically prevents the bones from scrapping against each other, and lacking it can cause a number of health problems. And I freely admit that is my on pedanticness poking at me with a sharp stick.

Still good enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: is this a boner joke

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: in situ: in the field, while in place, while active, etc

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After in situ analysis showed the remains to be nonanomalous, local police were notified of their presence.

Was this supposed to say site analysis?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't imagine a frowning skull.

Google images provides a couple examples of "frowning skull" graphics, like this one, which is apparently a pencil-holder:

+ show block
– hide block

Glad to hear you enjoyed the article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love it. Nothing like a creative skeleton skip.
One thing rattled my bones a bit: it says "smiling skull" in the description, but I can't imagine a frowning skull.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know how you did it, but you actually made spooky scary skeletons that send shivers down your spine interesting. +1

woohoohoohoo

thank you to everyone who enjoyed HEADBONES and found this humerus

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This sounds like something by Doctor Wondertainment.
Is it supposed to be?

Sorry; I just realised it said there wasn't anything about a manufacturer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this, the balance of somewhat silly and unnerving is my favorite type of skip, and you nailed it! The disclaimer in the letter at the end was an excellent touch as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DAMN! This was so good! One of my new favorites!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
product suggestion: Bluetooth

/me runs from angry mob

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The latter, since the product is "LUNGBUDS" after all.

Thanks for the +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quick question: When you say "alveoli", do you mean the place where the roots of your teeth are held, or the things in your lungs. I'm assuming you mean the latter, but I just want to make sure.

Other than that, this was pretty great. Plus One-d.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We worked pretty hard to find the balance of how much information to give, and there was definitely a fair bit of trimming involved.

Thanks for the comment and the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Head(bones)canon-ed. Now cannot unhear. Send help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something something can make your handbones clap

Glad you liked the article! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP remember me the weirdness of Series I, and enjoyed this a lot. The base concept is simple, but the article was flawless wroted, and is fun. Take my +1

Hear the song of the bones ♫ ♪

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if SCP-2902-1 tried them on? That could be interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a wonderfully weird article. I love the idea of trying to hear aroused skeletons to find human remains, and the other products made my laugh.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really spectacular. A unique concept delivered in a suitably unsettling manner. I particularly liked that you focused more on the object itself and not whatever corporation it was created by- merely hinting towards a larger scale operation. The ambiguity only lends to the skin-crawling feel.

Very enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is some classic, creepy, weird Series I shit right here and I love it. :D

Also, using these means YOU CAN HEAR THE BONES IN YOUR H A N D S

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my head, the voices for all the skeletons are now that of Skeletor from He-Man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting concept. Upvoted!

item.title: Re: Thank you all!
item.content:encoded:
Big, big thanks to everyone in the forums and chat who helped us out, especially in chat! There were a lot of tweaks we made to get the article to where it is now. And if you're here and you've read this, thank you too for reading!

I really enjoyed working with Lesh for this; pretty much all the addendum content is theirs! It's been a fair bit of time in the making, but I definitely think it was worthwhile!

item.title: Thank you all!
item.content:encoded:
Many thanks to Zyn for co-authoring this article! Her ideas and edits gave were invaluable for setting the tone and illustrating the object’s behavior. It was very kind of her to stick with me for this despite me being out of contact for months at a time. She also identified a butterfly I found.

Additional thanks to our magnificent reviewers:

  • In chat: taylor_itkin, Pecan, Hippo, MacLeod, Sly161, Kakroom, CaptainKirby
  • In forums: MrAnakinSpecter, minmin, WrongJohnSilver, MrWrong
  • In Wikidot PMs: AbsentmindedNihilist

Hopefully nobody has too many bones to pick with this skip.

The image is a derivative work I produced from a free stock image licensed under the CC0 Public Domain License.
